public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as watch sergeant.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a watch sergeant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of watch sergeant in and around Plume’s End Outpost, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Plume’s End Outpost workable through the obligations of watch sergeant while managing the immediate pressure of caravan disruption rumors in Shelk.
hidden_pressure: Must notice trouble early enough that force remains legitimate rather than merely reactive. Around Plume’s End Outpost, that usually means carrying caravan disruption rumors, celestial enforcer sightings, route law and storage discipline dominate. without letting the role of watch sergeant visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Any task accepted from Faelar Lightloom may turn into an escort, checkpoint, or route-control problem before the destination is reached.
Faelar Lightloom needs outside hands to handle a breach, seizure, or violent disturbance before it spreads through Plume’s End Outpost.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Faelar Lightloom as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named. In Plume’s End Outpost, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Roadwardens and caravan authorities.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Plume’s End Outpost: checkpoints, barracks, patrol seams, or visible trouble-spots
encounter_context.interaction_anchor: threshold control or rapid incident response
